message:
test(fcp-host): pin CapabilityToken typestate runtime contract — UnboundVerified→BoundVerified promotion + instance_id carriage + roundtrip identity (resolves flywheel_connectors-8zagc)

6 runtime tests in crates/fcp-host/tests/capability_token_typestate_runtime.rs
complementing jkcka_gateway_connector_handoff (high-level flow):

1. bound_token_carries_correct_instance_id_after_promotion — pin
   that the promoted bound token's CWT INSTANCE_ID claim string
   equals the connector's InstanceId::as_str() passed to
   promote_with_instance
2. bound_only_executor_accepts_promoted_token — type-level pin via
   a function whose signature demands &CapabilityToken<BoundVerified>;
   accepts promoted token; passing UnboundVerified is a compile error
3. unbound_and_bound_marker_types_are_distinct_at_type_level — runtime
   witness via std::any::TypeId showing the two type instantiations
   are distinct (PhantomData<Marker> shape enforces it)
4. unbound_to_bound_roundtrip_claims_match_direct_bound_verify —
   verify_unbound + promote_with_instance produces claims byte-for-byte
   identical to verify_bound (gateway split is implementation detail,
   not semantic divergence)
5. instance_agnostic_token_promotes_unconditionally — token with
   NO INSTANCE_ID claim promotes regardless of supplied InstanceId
   (matches verify_bound semantics; promotion does NOT synthesize
   the claim)
6. promote_with_wrong_instance_returns_structured_error — wrong
   instance produces structured FcpError (not panic)

Built and ran via rch exec — 6/6 green in 0.02s.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
